prerequisites: An Iterable account with access to the project you want to sync.
A Server-side API key with Standard permissions, created in the Iterable project you want to sync. Mobile, Browser, and other client-side API keys are not supported.
Knowledge of which data center hosts your Iterable project (US data center or European data center), since this must match the region config field.
A start_date value marking the earliest date from which to replicate data.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a single Iterable API key supplied in the api_key field. Use a Server-side key with Standard permissions. Each key only works against the data center that issued it, so it must match the region you configure.

setupSteps.title: Create a Server-side API key in Iterable
setupSteps.body: In Iterable, create a new API key of the Server-side type with Standard permissions, in the project you want to sync. Mobile, Browser, and other client-side keys are not supported. Copy the generated key value.

setupSteps.title: Identify your project's data center
setupSteps.body: Determine whether your Iterable project lives in the US data center (USDC) or the European data center (EDC). An API key only works against the data center that issued it, so the region you select must match. The connector calls https://api.iterable.com/api/ for US and https://api.eu.iterable.com/api/ for EU.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the key into the api_key field. Set region to US or EU to match your data center (defaults to US). Set the required start_date to the earliest date to replicate from. Optionally set lookback_window (in minutes) to re-read recently updated records on incremental runs.

sources.title: Iterable Source Connector docs (config fields, region values, required key type)
